non_canonical_notice: This registry is a derived recovery projection. It records dispositions for drift and does not change canon, glossary meaning, AGENTS.md, planning authority, schema law, ownership review, release policy, or protected doctrine.

authority_order.disposition: Wins over every lower semantic, execution, schema, validator, status, generated, and chat claim.
Wins for terminology and vocabulary meaning after the constitution.
Wins for repository execution discipline, validation expectations, work classes, protected areas, and agent governance below canon.
Classifies domain-specific authority and conflict resolution below root governance.
Defines intake, evidence, status, provenance, quarantine, and review handling below stronger sources.
Defines scoped semantic ownership for reviewed split roots below stronger canon and governance sources.
May guide recovery only when it agrees with all stronger sources and stays inside its declared scope.

drift_kinds.description: Lower prose, mirror docs, README text, audit notes, or status prose conflict with stronger doctrine.
A generated mirror, build echo, cache, run artifact, or projection no longer matches its source or lacks provenance.
Validator or fixture behavior conflicts with stronger doctrine, schema law, or registry projection boundaries.
Status, queue, latest-context, report, or audit state disagrees with current repo truth or allowed gate state.
Machine registry or projection diverges from the stronger document, schema law, or source it mirrors.
Multiple same-tier authoritative sources make materially incompatible claims in the same authority domain.
A projection, wrapper, transitional root, or scoped split is treated as the semantic owner by convenience.

recovery_actions.description: Use the higher authority source, record lower drift, and avoid mutation of protected canon or planning law.
Regenerate or edit a derived projection so it restates the stronger source without new meaning.
Align validator and fixture behavior to authority order, protected-root handling, recovery actions, and review gates.
Mark lower status, queue, report, or audit evidence stale or partial without claiming doctrine change.
Stop autonomous resolution, preserve conflicting candidates, and emit a bounded conflict packet for review.
Create a review packet when recovery would alter protected meaning, ownership, policy, release, trust, or public governance.
Reject attempts to treat generated, projected, transitional, status, chat, or validator evidence as canonical authority.

review_gates.trigger: The recovery edits only a lower derived artifact, stays inside explicit task scope, and does not change meaning.
review_gates.required_output: Validation evidence plus an audit statement that no protected doctrine meaning changed.

review_gates.trigger: The recovery would touch or reinterpret a protected root or protected planning/source document.
review_gates.required_output: Human review scope, touched protected paths, winning source, losing source, and reason autonomous recovery is insufficient.

review_gates.trigger: The recovery changes schema, contract, registry, validator-facing compatibility, migration, or refusal meaning.
review_gates.required_output: Contract impact statement, migration/refusal impact, schema parse evidence, and review decision if meaning changes.

review_gates.trigger: The recovery enters a split, transitional, projected, wrapper, or quarantined ownership family.
review_gates.required_output: Ownership packet naming canonical, projection, transitional, and quarantined surfaces with source paths.

review_gates.trigger: Same-tier conflict, insufficient evidence, material projection drift, or missing provenance prevents safe recovery.
review_gates.required_output: Conflict packet with authority domain, candidate paths, conflict reason, and minimal next review question.

review_gates.trigger: The recovery would change canon/glossary meaning, authority order, release/trust policy, public policy, or promote generated/status evidence.
review_gates.required_output: Explicit go/no-go review decision before mutation or promotion.

protected_roots.protection_reason: Canonical constitution and glossary meaning.
Root governance and agent execution contract.
Planning authority and conflict resolution procedure.
Snapshot intake, evidence, and quarantine law.
Merged execution-state and program continuity lock.
Extend-over-replace control surface.
Entry gates, exit proofs, and review checkpoints.
Reviewed ownership decisions for split and projection roots.
Reality semantic constitution specs.
Canonical schema law and migration/refusal obligations.
Release and publication doctrine.
Release artifacts, packaging, and update control-plane surfaces.
Repository control-plane and policy metadata.
Update channel and publication surfaces.
Trust and security policy surfaces.
Generated evidence only unless a stronger emission contract says otherwise.
Generated evidence and archived outputs with provenance requirements.
Cache output and generated evidence surface.
Run metadata and evidence output.
Foundation lock status surface explicitly forbidden for this recovery task.
Latest generated context surface explicitly forbidden for this recovery task.
Latest generated report surface explicitly forbidden for this recovery task.
Current queue status surface explicitly forbidden for this recovery task.

matrix.owned_meaning: Frozen semantic doctrine, vocabulary floor, root execution governance, validation expectations, and protected-area discipline.

drift_cases.when: A lower governance document or audit note contradicts canon, glossary, or AGENTS.md.
drift_cases.disposition: Use the higher source and repair only the lower artifact if the task scope allows it.

drift_cases.when: A validator accepts behavior forbidden by canon or AGENTS.md, such as silent semantic drift.
drift_cases.disposition: Repair the validator or fixture; escalate if contract meaning would change.

drift_cases.when: A status file claims a gate state or doctrine meaning not supported by repo sources.
drift_cases.disposition: Preserve repo truth and mark the lower status stale or partial where edits are allowed.

matrix.surface_label: Planning authority, intake, gates, and program state
matrix.owned_meaning: Planning intake law, authority ranking, gates, exit proofs, sequence locks, and extend-over-replace discipline.

drift_cases.when: A machine planning mirror or generated task projection disagrees with planning intake or gate law.
drift_cases.disposition: Refresh the lower projection if its source is clear; quarantine missing-provenance mirrors.

drift_cases.when: Two protected planning surfaces make incompatible current-state or gate claims.
drift_cases.disposition: Stop autonomous resolution and emit the conflict packet required by intake law.

drift_cases.when: A queue, report, or latest-context state conflicts with the committed planning state.
drift_cases.disposition: Do not rewrite forbidden latest files; cite repo truth and record staleness in an allowed audit surface.

matrix.owned_meaning: Schema identity, versioning, compatibility, migration, refusal, defaulting, and contract-facing meaning.

drift_cases.when: A registry or projection differs from stronger schema law or contract documentation.
drift_cases.disposition: Restore projection lineage or quarantine the material disagreement.

drift_cases.when: A validator normalizes schema drift, silent defaults, or implicit migrations.
drift_cases.disposition: Repair the validator to require explicit version, migration, refusal, and proof semantics.

matrix.owned_meaning: Derived governance recovery, policy, and adapter projection surfaces that must remain subordinate to canon and AGENTS.md.

drift_cases.when: A governance projection claims canonical status or conflicts with AGENTS.md.
drift_cases.disposition: Refresh the projection and keep the non-canonical notice explicit.

drift_cases.when: A generated governance mirror becomes stale or loses source provenance.
drift_cases.disposition: Refresh from the canonical source or quarantine if source lineage is unknown.

matrix.owned_meaning: Scoped semantic ownership, projection, wrapper, transitional, and residual quarantine boundaries for split roots.

drift_cases.when: A task binds to a projection, wrapper, or transitional root as semantic owner by convenience.
drift_cases.disposition: Apply reviewed scoped ownership and route unresolved substitution or convergence to review.

drift_cases.when: Scoped owners make incompatible claims and no stronger source chooses a winner.
drift_cases.disposition: Quarantine the subject and preserve both candidates for review.

matrix.surface_label: Generated evidence and run output surfaces
matrix.owned_meaning: Evidence of generated or run-produced state, never semantic doctrine without explicit stronger promotion.

drift_cases.when: Generated output is stale, unprovenanced, or contradicted by source documents.
drift_cases.disposition: Treat as evidence only, refresh through the emitter if in scope, or quarantine the evidence.

drift_cases.when: Run metadata claims pass/fail or gate status that stronger current evidence does not support.
drift_cases.disposition: Record staleness and avoid promoting run metadata into authority.

matrix.owned_meaning: Validation mechanics and fixtures that enforce, but do not create, doctrine and contract meaning.

drift_cases.when: A validator omits authority order, protected roots, recovery actions, or review gates.
drift_cases.disposition: Repair validator logic and fixtures to make those recovery requirements explicit.

drift_cases.when: A test fixture or registry shape stops matching its schema or documented disposition matrix.
drift_cases.disposition: Refresh the lower fixture or registry without changing stronger doctrine meaning.

matrix.owned_meaning: Status, audit, report, and queue evidence that records state but does not outrank repo authority.

drift_cases.when: A status or audit surface claims current progress, gate closure, or validation not supported by repo evidence.
drift_cases.disposition: Preserve repo truth, record staleness in an allowed audit note, and avoid forbidden latest-file edits.

drift_cases.when: An audit or status document is mistaken for a canonical doctrine source.
drift_cases.disposition: Cite the stronger source and keep the audit/status document derived.
